title: Eyes on arts: Upcoming entertainment highlights
bodyText:
GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Georgians can pick and choose their weekend entertainment from a large variety of events.Below is our weekly calendar for the arts: Eyes on arts. These entries are sent to George Herald on a weekly basis. Have a look and book your tickets for some stunning entertainment on your doorstep.


> 26 April - 17 May:


Wild at Attic Atelier, 33 CJ Langenhoven Road. An exhibition of artworks created during a workshop of the CAP Institute for Contemporary Art Practice. Natural materials were used to create these works, inspired by an engagement with wild nature in the George plantation.


> 2-3 May:


Triple Bill of Dance (19:00) at the Market Theatre. Cloud 9 & Elvinia's Dance Academy presents three varied danceworks: Dance Ballads, Voices in My Head and French Collection. Tickets cost R120 per person: send a WhatsApp to Elvinia on 083 725 4215.

Richelle Baird (16) and Kayla Hone (17, left) in action on stage during rehearsals for Triple Bill of Dance (2-3 May).


> 2-10 May:


Chekhov Moments (varied times) at the George Arts Theatre. George Society of Arts presents this year's Tapas Theatre, a mesmerising selection of Anton Chekhov's most compelling scenes in which unspoken desires, quiet tragedies and fleeting joys come to life. With a delicate balance of comedy and melancholy, this collection invites audiences to witness Chekhov's world of raw and poetic beauty through these complex, riveting characters. Showtimes are 19:00 on 2 May; 14:00 and 19:00 on 3 May; 19:00 on 9 May; 14:00 and 19:00 on 4 May. Tickets are available on www.georgeartstheatre.co.za. Price: R200 (including food and drink), R140 (excluding food and drink).


> 4 May:


Carpe Musicam! Pops Band presents Rock, Pop & Romance at DRC Sedgefield. Tickets cost R80 (adults) and R20 (scholars) and are available at www.carpemusicam.co.za or at the door (cash only).

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
